$comment: Shared, language-neutral SKILL catalog for the cross-SDK E2E — the on-demand analogue of fixtures/catalog.json. Skills are ranked by the native SkillRegistry (a separate dense corpus from tools), surfaced in the `skills` bucket of search_capabilities, and loaded by id via get_skill_content. Descriptions are distinctive so each scenario query has an unambiguous top-1 within the skill corpus. Most skills set only id/name/description/body; `deploy-web-service` also declares a `tools` dependency, which search_capabilities surfaces into the tools bucket (score 0) when the skill matches — the cross-pollination case. The TS and Python Skill types are in parity (id, name, description, tags, tools, metadata, body — all mirror the same Rust core struct, and `tools`/`metadata` are dependency/ranking signals, NOT indexed query terms), so these fields behave identically across SDKs. Extend alongside e2e/scenario.json. See e2e/README.md.

skills.name: deploy-web-service
skills.description: Playbook for safely deploying the web service to the production environment, with health checks and a rollback procedure.
skills.body:
# Deploy the web service
1. Tag the release.
2. Roll out behind a health check.
3. If the health check fails, roll back.

skills.name: debug-flaky-tests
skills.description: How to diagnose and stabilize flaky, intermittently failing automated tests in continuous integration.
skills.body:
# Stabilize flaky tests
1. Reproduce under load.
2. Quarantine the offending test.
3. Remove the source of nondeterminism.

skills.name: speed-up-slow-query
skills.description: Playbook for speeding up a slow Postgres query by adding the right index, run safely in production.
skills.body:
# Speed up a slow query
1. EXPLAIN the query.
2. Add a covering index concurrently.
3. Re-check the query plan.

skills.name: onboard-new-engineer
skills.description: Checklist for onboarding a newly hired engineer: provisioning accounts, granting repository access, and assigning a first task.
skills.body:
# Onboard a new engineer
1. Provision accounts.
2. Grant repository access.
3. Assign a starter task.
